17	#  greeting_enabled              :boolean          default(FALSE)
18	#  greeting_message              :string
19	#  lock_to_single_conversation   :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
20	#  name                          :string           not null
21	#  out_of_office_message         :string
22	#  sender_name_type              :integer          default("friendly"), not null
23	#  timezone                      :string           default("UTC")
24	#  working_hours_enabled         :boolean          default(FALSE)
25	#  created_at                    :datetime         not null
26	#  updated_at                    :datetime         not null

30	#
31	# Indexes

33	#  index_inboxes_on_account_id                   (account_id)
34	#  index_inboxes_on_channel_id_and_channel_type  (channel_id,channel_type)
35	#  index_inboxes_on_portal_id                    (portal_id)
36	#
37	# Foreign Keys
38	#
39	#  fk_rails_...  (portal_id => portals.id)
40	#
41	
42	class Inbox < ApplicationRecord
43	  include Reportable
44	  include Avatarable
45	  include OutOfOffisable
46	  include AccountCacheRevalidator
47	  include InboxAgentAvailability
48	
49	  # Not allowing characters:
50	  validates :name, presence: true
51	  validates :account_id, presence: true
52	  validates :timezone, inclusion: { in: TZInfo::Timezone.all_identifiers }
53	  validates :out_of_office_message, length: { maximum: Limits::OUT_OF_OFFICE_MESSAGE_MAX_LENGTH }
54	  validates :greeting_message, length: { maximum: Limits::GREETING_MESSAGE_MAX_LENGTH }
55	  validate :ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit
56	
57	  belongs_to :account
58	  belongs_to :portal, optional: true
59	
60	  belongs_to :channel, polymorphic: true, dependent: :destroy
61	
62	  has_many :campaigns, dependent: :destroy_async
63	  has_many :contact_inboxes, dependent: :destroy_async
64	  has_many :contacts, through: :contact_inboxes
65	
66	  has_many :inbox_members, dependent: :destroy_async
67	  has_many :members, through: :inbox_members, source: :user
68	  has_many :conversations, dependent: :destroy_async
69	  has_many :messages, dependent: :destroy_async
70	
71	  has_one :inbox_assignment_policy, dependent: :destroy
72	  has_one :assignment_policy, through: :inbox_assignment_policy
73	  has_one :agent_bot_inbox, dependent: :destroy_async
74	  has_one :agent_bot, through: :agent_bot_inbox
75	  has_many :webhooks, dependent: :destroy_async
76	  has_many :hooks, dependent: :destroy_async, class_name: 'Integrations::Hook'
77	
78	  enum sender_name_type: { friendly: 0, professional: 1 }
79	
80	  after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents
81	
82	  after_create_commit :dispatch_create_event
83	  after_update_commit :dispatch_update_event
84	
85	  scope :order_by_name, -> { order('lower(name) ASC') }
86	
87	  # Adds multiple members to the inbox
88	  # @param user_ids [Array<Integer>] Array of user IDs to add as members
89	  # @return [void]
90	  def add_members(user_ids)
91	    inbox_members.create!(user_ids.map { |user_id| { user_id: user_id } })
92	    update_account_cache
93	  end
94	
95	  # Removes multiple members from the inbox
96	  # @param user_ids [Array<Integer>] Array of user IDs to remove
97	  # @return [void]
98	  def remove_members(user_ids)
99	    inbox_members.where(user_id: user_ids).destroy_all
100	    update_account_cache
101	  end
102	
103	  # Sanitizes inbox name for balanced email provider compatibility
104	  # ALLOWS: /'._- and Unicode letters/numbers/emojis
105	  # REMOVES: Forbidden chars (\<>@"()) + spam-trigger symbols (!#$%&*+=?^`{|}~)
106	  def sanitized_name
107	    return default_name_for_blank_name if name.blank?
108	
109	    sanitized = apply_sanitization_rules(name)
110	    sanitized.blank? && email? ? display_name_from_email : sanitized
111	  end
112	
113	  def sanitized_business_name
114	    sanitize_raw_name(business_name) || sanitized_name
115	  end
116	
117	  def sms?
118	    channel_type == 'Channel::Sms'
119	  end
120	
121	  def facebook?
122	    channel_type == 'Channel::FacebookPage'
123	  end
124	
125	  def instagram?
126	    (facebook? || instagram_direct?) && channel.instagram_id.present?
127	  end
128	
129	  def instagram_direct?
130	    channel_type == 'Channel::Instagram'
131	  end
132	
133	  def tiktok?
134	    channel_type == 'Channel::Tiktok'
135	  end
136	
137	  def web_widget?
138	    channel_type == 'Channel::WebWidget'
139	  end
140	
141	  def api?
142	    channel_type == 'Channel::Api'
143	  end
144	
145	  def email?
146	    channel_type == 'Channel::Email'
147	  end
148	
149	  def twilio?
150	    channel_type == 'Channel::TwilioSms'
151	  end
152	
153	  def twitter?
154	    channel_type == 'Channel::TwitterProfile'
155	  end
156	
157	  def telegram?
158	    channel_type == 'Channel::Telegram'
159	  end
160	
161	  def whatsapp?
162	    channel_type == 'Channel::Whatsapp'
163	  end
164	
165	  def twilio_whatsapp?
166	    channel_type == 'Channel::TwilioSms' && channel.medium == 'whatsapp'
167	  end
168	
169	  def assignable_agents
170	    (account.users.where(id: members.select(:user_id)) + account.administrators).uniq
171	  end
172	
173	  def active_bot?
174	    agent_bot_inbox&.active? || hooks.where(app_id: %w[dialogflow],
175	                                            status: 'enabled').count.positive?
176	  end
177	
178	  def inbox_type
179	    channel.name
180	  end
181	
182	  def webhook_data
183	    {
184	      id: id,
185	      name: name
186	    }
187	  end
188	
189	  def callback_webhook_url
190	    case channel_type
191	    when 'Channel::TwilioSms'
192	      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/twilio/callback"
193	    when 'Channel::Sms'
194	      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/sms/#{channel.phone_number.delete_prefix('+')}"
195	    when 'Channel::Line'
196	      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/line/#{channel.line_channel_id}"
197	    when 'Channel::Whatsapp'
198	      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/whatsapp/#{channel.phone_number}"
199	    end
200	  end
201	
202	  def member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
203	    members.ids
204	  end
205	
206	  def auto_assignment_v2_enabled?
207	    account.feature_enabled?('assignment_v2')
208	  end
209	
210	  # Callers (Reauthorizable) only invoke this on a real transition, so the previous
211	  # value is always the inverse of the new boolean value.
212	  def dispatch_reauthorization_event(reauthorization_required)
213	    return if ENV['ENABLE_INBOX_EVENTS'].blank?
214	
215	    changed_attributes = { reauthorization_required: [!reauthorization_required, reauthorization_required] }
216	    Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(INBOX_UPDATED, Time.zone.now, inbox: self, changed_attributes: changed_attributes)
217	  end
218	
219	  private
220	
221	  def default_name_for_blank_name
222	    email? ? display_name_from_email : ''
223	  end
224	
225	  def sanitize_raw_name(raw)
226	    return nil if raw.blank?
227	
228	    result = apply_sanitization_rules(raw)
229	    result.presence
230	  end
231	
232	  def apply_sanitization_rules(name)
233	    name.gsub(/[\\<>@"!#$%&*+=?^`{|}~:;()]/, '')        # Remove forbidden chars
234	        .gsub(/[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/, ' ')                   # Replace control chars with spaces
235	        .gsub(/\A[[:punct:]]+|[[:punct:]]+\z/, '')      # Remove leading/trailing punctuation
236	        .gsub(/\s+/, ' ')                               # Normalize spaces
237	        .strip

# frozen_string_literal: true

# == Schema Information
#
# Table name: inboxes
#
#  id                            :integer          not null, primary key
#  allow_messages_after_resolved :boolean          default(TRUE)
#  auto_assignment_config        :jsonb
#  business_name                 :string
#  channel_type                  :string
#  csat_config                   :jsonb            not null
#  csat_survey_enabled           :boolean          default(FALSE)
#  email_address                 :string
#  enable_auto_assignment        :boolean          default(TRUE)
#  enable_email_collect          :boolean          default(TRUE)
#  greeting_enabled              :boolean          default(FALSE)
#  greeting_message              :string
#  lock_to_single_conversation   :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
#  name                          :string           not null
#  out_of_office_message         :string
#  sender_name_type              :integer          default("friendly"), not null
#  timezone                      :string           default("UTC")
#  working_hours_enabled         :boolean          default(FALSE)
#  created_at                    :datetime         not null
#  updated_at                    :datetime         not null
#  account_id                    :integer          not null
#  channel_id                    :integer          not null
#  portal_id                     :bigint
#
# Indexes
#
#  index_inboxes_on_account_id                   (account_id)
#  index_inboxes_on_channel_id_and_channel_type  (channel_id,channel_type)
#  index_inboxes_on_portal_id                    (portal_id)
#
# Foreign Keys
#
#  fk_rails_...  (portal_id => portals.id)
#

class Inbox < ApplicationRecord
  include Reportable
  include Avatarable
  include OutOfOffisable
  include AccountCacheRevalidator
  include InboxAgentAvailability

  # Not allowing characters:
  validates :name, presence: true
  validates :account_id, presence: true
  validates :timezone, inclusion: { in: TZInfo::Timezone.all_identifiers }
  validates :out_of_office_message, length: { maximum: Limits::OUT_OF_OFFICE_MESSAGE_MAX_LENGTH }
  validates :greeting_message, length: { maximum: Limits::GREETING_MESSAGE_MAX_LENGTH }
  validate :ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit

  belongs_to :account
  belongs_to :portal, optional: true

  belongs_to :channel, polymorphic: true, dependent: :destroy

  has_many :campaigns, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_many :contact_inboxes, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_many :contacts, through: :contact_inboxes

  has_many :inbox_members, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_many :members, through: :inbox_members, source: :user
  has_many :conversations, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_many :messages, dependent: :destroy_async

  has_one :inbox_assignment_policy, dependent: :destroy
  has_one :assignment_policy, through: :inbox_assignment_policy
  has_one :agent_bot_inbox, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_one :agent_bot, through: :agent_bot_inbox
  has_many :webhooks, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_many :hooks, dependent: :destroy_async, class_name: 'Integrations::Hook'

  enum sender_name_type: { friendly: 0, professional: 1 }

  after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents

  after_create_commit :dispatch_create_event
  after_update_commit :dispatch_update_event

  scope :order_by_name, -> { order('lower(name) ASC') }

  # Adds multiple members to the inbox
  # @param user_ids [Array<Integer>] Array of user IDs to add as members
  # @return [void]
  def add_members(user_ids)
    inbox_members.create!(user_ids.map { |user_id| { user_id: user_id } })
    update_account_cache
  end

  # Removes multiple members from the inbox
  # @param user_ids [Array<Integer>] Array of user IDs to remove
  # @return [void]
  def remove_members(user_ids)
    inbox_members.where(user_id: user_ids).destroy_all
    update_account_cache
  end

  # Sanitizes inbox name for balanced email provider compatibility
  # ALLOWS: /'._- and Unicode letters/numbers/emojis
  # REMOVES: Forbidden chars (\<>@"()) + spam-trigger symbols (!#$%&*+=?^`{|}~)
  def sanitized_name
    return default_name_for_blank_name if name.blank?

    sanitized = apply_sanitization_rules(name)
    sanitized.blank? && email? ? display_name_from_email : sanitized
  end

  def sanitized_business_name
    sanitize_raw_name(business_name) || sanitized_name
  end

  def sms?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Sms'
  end

  def facebook?
    channel_type == 'Channel::FacebookPage'
  end

  def instagram?
    (facebook? || instagram_direct?) && channel.instagram_id.present?
  end

  def instagram_direct?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Instagram'
  end

  def tiktok?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Tiktok'
  end

  def web_widget?
    channel_type == 'Channel::WebWidget'
  end

  def api?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Api'
  end

  def email?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Email'
  end

  def twilio?
    channel_type == 'Channel::TwilioSms'
  end

  def twitter?
    channel_type == 'Channel::TwitterProfile'
  end

  def telegram?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Telegram'
  end

  def whatsapp?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Whatsapp'
  end

  def twilio_whatsapp?
    channel_type == 'Channel::TwilioSms' && channel.medium == 'whatsapp'
  end

  def assignable_agents
    (account.users.where(id: members.select(:user_id)) + account.administrators).uniq
  end

  def active_bot?
    agent_bot_inbox&.active? || hooks.where(app_id: %w[dialogflow],
                                            status: 'enabled').count.positive?
  end

  def inbox_type
    channel.name
  end

  def webhook_data
    {
      id: id,
      name: name
    }
  end

  def callback_webhook_url
    case channel_type
    when 'Channel::TwilioSms'
      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/twilio/callback"
    when 'Channel::Sms'
      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/sms/#{channel.phone_number.delete_prefix('+')}"
    when 'Channel::Line'
      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/line/#{channel.line_channel_id}"
    when 'Channel::Whatsapp'
      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/whatsapp/#{channel.phone_number}"
    end
  end

  def member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
    members.ids
  end

  def auto_assignment_v2_enabled?
    account.feature_enabled?('assignment_v2')
  end

  # Callers (Reauthorizable) only invoke this on a real transition, so the previous
  # value is always the inverse of the new boolean value.
  def dispatch_reauthorization_event(reauthorization_required)
    return if ENV['ENABLE_INBOX_EVENTS'].blank?

    changed_attributes = { reauthorization_required: [!reauthorization_required, reauthorization_required] }
    Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(INBOX_UPDATED, Time.zone.now, inbox: self, changed_attributes: changed_attributes)
  end

  private

  def default_name_for_blank_name
    email? ? display_name_from_email : ''
  end

  def sanitize_raw_name(raw)
    return nil if raw.blank?

    result = apply_sanitization_rules(raw)
    result.presence
  end

  def apply_sanitization_rules(name)
    name.gsub(/[\\<>@"!#$%&*+=?^`{|}~:;()]/, '')        # Remove forbidden chars
        .gsub(/[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/, ' ')                   # Replace control chars with spaces
        .gsub(/\A[[:punct:]]+|[[:punct:]]+\z/, '')      # Remove leading/trailing punctuation
        .gsub(/\s+/, ' ')                               # Normalize spaces
        .strip
  end

  def display_name_from_email
    channel.email.split('@').first.parameterize.titleize
  end

  def dispatch_create_event
    return if ENV['ENABLE_INBOX_EVENTS'].blank?

    Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(INBOX_CREATED, Time.zone.now, inbox: self)
  end

  def dispatch_update_event
    return if ENV['ENABLE_INBOX_EVENTS'].blank?

    Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(INBOX_UPDATED, Time.zone.now, inbox: self, changed_attributes: previous_changes)
  end

  def ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit
    # overridden in enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox.rb
  end

  def delete_round_robin_agents
    ::AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService.new(inbox: self).clear_queue
  end

  def check_channel_type?
    ['Channel::Email', 'Channel::Api', 'Channel::WebWidget'].include?(channel_type)
  end
end

Inbox.prepend_mod_with('Inbox')
Inbox.include_mod_with('Audit::Inbox')
Inbox.include_mod_with('Concerns::Inbox')

module Enterprise::Inbox
  def member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
    return super unless enable_auto_assignment?
    return filter_by_capacity(available_agents).map(&:user_id) if auto_assignment_v2_enabled?

    max_assignment_limit = auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit']
    overloaded_agent_ids = max_assignment_limit.present? ? get_agent_ids_over_assignment_limit(max_assignment_limit) : []
    super - overloaded_agent_ids
  end

  def active_bot?
    super || captain_active?
  end

  def captain_active?
    captain_assistant.present? && more_responses?
  end

  private

  def more_responses?
    account.usage_limits[:captain][:responses][:current_available].positive?
  end

  def get_agent_ids_over_assignment_limit(limit)
    conversations
      .open
      .where(account_id: account_id)
      .select(:assignee_id)
      .group(:assignee_id)
      .having("count(*) >= #{limit.to_i}")
      .filter_map(&:assignee_id)
  end

  def ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit
    return if auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit'].blank?
    return if auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit'].to_i.positive?

    errors.add(:auto_assignment_config, 'max_assignment_limit must be greater than 0')
  end
end

     1	class AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob < ApplicationJob
     2	  queue_as :default
     3	
     4	  IN_FLIGHT_TTL = 5.minutes
     5	
     6	  # Coalesce per inbox: at most one AssignmentJob per inbox is in-flight
     7	  # (queued or running) at any time. The marker carries a token so a job only
     8	  # releases its own claim (a newer job may have taken it after a TTL lapse).
     9	  def self.enqueue_for_inbox(inbox_id)
    10	    key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
    11	    token = SecureRandom.uuid
    12	    return false unless ::Redis::Alfred.set(key, token, nx: true, ex: IN_FLIGHT_TTL)
    13	
    14	    return true if perform_later(inbox_id: inbox_id, token: token)
    15	
    16	    # Enqueue was halted; release our own claim so the inbox isn't gated until the TTL.
    17	    ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
    18	    false
    19	  rescue StandardError
    20	    # Enqueue raised after we claimed the gate; release our own claim, then re-raise.
    21	    ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
    22	    raise
    23	  end
    24	
    25	  def perform(inbox_id:, token: nil)
    26	    inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id)
    27	    return unless inbox
    28	
    29	    service = AutoAssignment::AssignmentService.new(inbox: inbox)
    30	    assigned_count = service.perform_bulk_assignment(limit: bulk_assignment_limit)
    31	    Rails.logger.info "Assigned #{assigned_count} conversations for inbox #{inbox.id}"
    32	  rescue StandardError => e
    33	    Rails.logger.error "Bulk assignment failed for inbox #{inbox_id}: #{e.message}"
    34	    raise e if Rails.env.test?
    35	  ensure
    36	    release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
    37	  end
    38	
    39	  private
    40	
    41	  # Release the in-flight marker only if we still own it. The atomic
    42	  # compare-and-delete ensures a job whose TTL lapsed can't delete a newer
    43	  # job's claim. Tokenless (pre-deploy) jobs never claimed a key, so skip.
    44	  def release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
    45	    return if token.nil?
    46	
    47	    key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
    48	    ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
    49	  end
    50	
    51	  def bulk_assignment_limit
    52	    ENV.fetch('AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_BULK_LIMIT', 100).to_i
    53	  end
    54	end

I now have a complete picture. Here is the audit, step by step.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Chatwoot, a Rails (backend) + Vue 3 (frontend) customer-engagement / shared-inbox platform. Standard Rails layout under `app/` (`models`, `controllers`, `services`, `jobs`, `listeners`, `builders`, `finders`, `policies`, `dispatchers`), library code under `lib/`, and a **paid-tier Enterprise overlay under `enterprise/`** that mirrors the same tree and extends OSS via `prepend_mod_with`/`include_mod_with`. Frontend lives in `app/javascript`. Specs in `spec/` (OSS) and `spec/enterprise/`.

**The model you're reworking — `Inbox`** — `app/models/inbox.rb:42`. An Inbox is the join between an **external messaging channel** (`belongs_to :channel, polymorphic: true, dependent: :destroy`, `inbox.rb:60`; the 12 channel classes live in `app/models/channel/`) and the rest of the system: conversations, contacts, messages, members all hang off it. It belongs to an `Account` (`inbox.rb:57`) and optionally a `Portal` (`inbox.rb:58`).

**Shared pieces it is built on (the concerns it includes):**
- `Reportable` — `app/models/concerns/reportable.rb:3` (adds `has_many :reporting_events`)
- `Avatarable` — `app/models/concerns/avatarable.rb:3`
- `OutOfOffisable` — `app/models/concerns/out_of_offisable.rb:3` (adds `has_many :working_hours` + default-hours creation)
- `AccountCacheRevalidator` — `app/models/concerns/account_cache_revalidator.rb:1`
- `InboxAgentAvailability` — `app/models/concerns/inbox_agent_availability.rb:1`

**Enterprise overlay of the model:** `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox.rb:1`, `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/concerns/inbox.rb:1` (adds `captain_inbox`, `inbox_capacity_limits`, `calls`), `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/audit/inbox.rb:1`, wired at `inbox.rb:269-271`.

---

## Step 2 — The Inbox contract and what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/inbox.rb:42`.

**Concerns it pulls in (shared behavior):**
| Concern | file:line | What it injects |
|---|---|---|
| `Reportable` | `app/models/concerns/reportable.rb:7` | `has_many :reporting_events, dependent: :destroy` |
| `Avatarable` | `app/models/concerns/avatarable.rb:10` | `has_one_attached :avatar`, gravatar fetch on save |
| `OutOfOffisable` | `app/models/concerns/out_of_offisable.rb:9-10` | `has_many :working_hours, dependent: :destroy_async`; `after_create :create_default_working_hours` |
| `AccountCacheRevalidator` | `app/models/concerns/account_cache_revalidator.rb:5` | `after_commit :update_account_cache` on create/update/**destroy** |
| `InboxAgentAvailability` | `app/models/concerns/inbox_agent_availability.rb` | `available_agents`, `member_ids_with_assignment_capacity` (overridden in EE at `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox_agent_availability.rb:4`) |

**Associations other records hang off it** (`inbox.rb:57-76`):
- `belongs_to :channel, polymorphic:, dependent: :destroy` (`:60`) — and the reverse `Channelable` side declares `has_one :inbox, as: :channel, dependent: :destroy_async, touch: true` (`app/models/concerns/channelable.rb:6`).
- `has_many :campaigns, :contact_inboxes, :conversations, :messages, :inbox_members, :webhooks, :hooks` — **all `dependent: :destroy_async`** (`:62-76`).
- `has_one :inbox_assignment_policy, dependent: :destroy` (`:71`), `has_one :agent_bot_inbox, dependent: :destroy_async` (`:73`).
- `has_many :contacts, through: :contact_inboxes` (`:64`), `has_many :members, through: :inbox_members` (`:67`).
- EE adds `has_one :captain_inbox, dependent: :destroy`, `has_many :inbox_capacity_limits, dependent: :destroy`, `has_many :calls, dependent: :destroy_async` (`enterprise/app/models/enterprise/concerns/inbox.rb:5-10`).

**Lifecycle callbacks (part of the contract):**
- `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` → clears the Redis round-robin queue (`inbox.rb:80, 260-262`).
- `after_create_commit :dispatch_create_event` / `after_update_commit :dispatch_update_event` (`inbox.rb:82-83`), plus `dispatch_reauthorization_event` (`inbox.rb:212`).
- EE: `audited associated_with: :account` (`enterprise/app/models/enterprise/audit/inbox.rb:5`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** teardown is split across **three mechanisms with different timing** — `:destroy` (inline, synchronous: channel, `inbox_assignment_policy`), `:destroy_async` (enqueued per-record: conversations, messages, contact_inboxes, campaigns, members, webhooks, hooks, working_hours, agent_bot_inbox, calls), and an `after_destroy` Redis side-effect. `reporting_events` is `:destroy` via the concern but **also batch-purged by `DeleteObjectJob`** (see Step 3). The channel relationship is **bidirectional `dependent: :destroy`** — destroying either side cascades to the other, so the entry point matters.

---

## Step 3 — Teardown trace (inbox + attached data)

**Entry points (where destroy is triggered):**
1. **Single inbox deletion** — `app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/inboxes_controller.rb:77-78`: `DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(@inbox, Current.user, request.ip)`.
2. **Account teardown** — `app/controllers/super_admin/accounts_controller.rb:60-63`: `DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(account)` (account `has_many :inboxes, dependent: :destroy_async`, `app/models/account.rb:83`).
3. **Channel deletion** cascades to its inbox via `Channelable` `has_one :inbox, dependent: :destroy_async` (`app/models/concerns/channelable.rb:6`).

**Layers, in order:**
1. `DeleteObjectJob#perform` — `app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb:6-12`. Runs on the `:low` queue.
2. `purge_heavy_associations` — `delete_object_job.rb:25-34`. For an `Inbox`, **inline batch-destroys** `conversations`, `contact_inboxes`, `reporting_events` in batches of 5,000 (`heavy_associations` map `:18-23`, `batch_destroy` `:36-40`). This pre-empties the largest tables synchronously *before* the model destroy, to avoid `destroy_async` fan-out timeouts/races. (For an `Account` it purges `conversations, contacts, inboxes, reporting_events`; EE adds `SlaPolicy => [:applied_slas]` at `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb:4-8`.)
3. `object.destroy!` — `delete_object_job.rb:10`. Fires AR `dependent:` cascades:
   - **Inline (`:destroy`)**: `channel` (`inbox.rb:60`), `inbox_assignment_policy` (`:71`), and EE `captain_inbox`/`inbox_capacity_limits` (`enterprise/.../concerns/inbox.rb:5,9`), plus `reporting_events` (`reportable.rb:7`).
   - **Async (`:destroy_async`, enqueued one job per association)**: `campaigns, contact_inboxes, conversations, messages, inbox_members, webhooks, hooks` (`inbox.rb:62-76`), `agent_bot_inbox` (`:73`), `working_hours` (`out_of_offisable.rb:9`), EE `calls`.
   - `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` → `AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService#clear_queue` deletes the Redis key inline (`inbox.rb:260-262`, `app/services/auto_assignment/inbox_round_robin_service.rb:5-7`).
   - `after_commit :update_account_cache` bumps the account cache key (`account_cache_revalidator.rb:5-9`).
4. `process_post_deletion_tasks` — `delete_object_job.rb:11,14` (no-op in OSS). EE override writes an `Enterprise::AuditLog` `destroy` entry for Inbox/Conversation/SlaPolicy (`enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb:10-25`).

**Async vs inline:** Inline = the heavy pre-purge (conversations/contact_inboxes/reporting_events), the polymorphic `channel`, `inbox_assignment_policy`, EE captain/capacity, and the Redis queue clear. Async (separate jobs) = messages, members, campaigns, webhooks, hooks, agent_bot_inbox, working_hours, calls, and the *normal* (non-pre-purged) path for contact_inboxes/conversations when destroyed outside `DeleteObjectJob`.

---

## Step 4 — Exhaustive dependent audit (grouped by area)

### A. Models — direct `belongs_to :inbox` (FK dependents)
| file:line | use |
|---|---|
| `app/models/conversation.rb:101` | core: every conversation belongs to an inbox (`inbox_id` NOT NULL, `:66`) |
| `app/models/message.rb:129` | every message belongs to an inbox |
| `app/models/contact_inbox.rb:32` | contact↔inbox join (validates twilio/whatsapp source_id against `inbox.channel`, `:60-77`) |
| `app/models/campaign.rb:45` | campaigns target an inbox |
| `app/models/webhook.rb:23` | optional inbox-scoped webhook |
| `app/models/working_hour.rb:24` | inbox business hours |
| `app/models/agent_bot_inbox.rb:19` | bot↔inbox join |
| `app/models/reporting_event.rb:37` | optional, analytics events |
| `app/models/integrations/hook.rb:40` | optional, integration hooks |
| `app/models/inbox_member.rb:23` | agent membership; round-robin side-effects (`:25-36`) |
| `app/models/inbox_assignment_policy.rb:17` | inbox↔policy join |
| `enterprise/app/models/inbox_capacity_limit.rb:20` | EE capacity per inbox |
| `enterprise/app/models/call.rb:46` | EE voice calls |
| `enterprise/app/models/sla_event.rb:26` | EE SLA events |
| `enterprise/app/models/captain_inbox.rb:19` | EE Captain↔inbox join |

### B. Models — reverse / through / shared-concern dependents (indirect — text search misses these)
| file:line | use |
|---|---|
| `app/models/account.rb:83` | `has_many :inboxes, dependent: :destroy_async` (account teardown driver) |
| `app/models/concerns/channelable.rb:6` | every `Channel::*` declares `has_one :inbox, dependent: :destroy_async, touch:` |
| `app/models/user.rb:99-100` | `inbox_members` / `inboxes through:` |
| `app/models/agent_bot.rb:33` | `inboxes through: agent_bot_inboxes` |
| `app/models/contact.rb:62` | `inboxes through: contact_inboxes` |
| `app/models/portal.rb:38` | `has_many :inboxes, dependent: :nullify` (help-center portal) |
| `app/models/assignment_policy.rb:25-26` | `inboxes through: inbox_assignment_policies` |
| `enterprise/app/models/agent_capacity_policy.rb:21-22` | `inboxes through: inbox_capacity_limits` |
| `enterprise/app/models/captain/assistant.rb:33` | `has_many :inboxes` |
| `app/models/concerns/auto_assignment_handler.rb:11-47` | Conversation `after_save` reads `inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled?`, `enable_auto_assignment?`, `member_ids_with_assignment_capacity`, `members` |
| `app/models/concerns/assignment_handler.rb` | conversation assignment via inbox members |

### C. Request handling (controllers)
Inbox CRUD + nested resources: `app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/inboxes_controller.rb` (destroy `:77`), `inbox_members_controller.rb`, `inbox_csat_templates_controller.rb`, `inboxes/assignment_policies_controller.rb`, `assignment_policies/inboxes_controller.rb`, `contact_inboxes_controller.rb`, `conversations_controller.rb`, `webhooks_controller.rb`, `campaigns_controller.rb`, `csat_survey_responses_controller.rb`, `channels/twilio_channels_controller.rb`, `concerns/whatsapp_health_management.rb`. Public/widget surface: `public/api/v1/inboxes_controller.rb` + `inboxes/{messages,conversations,contacts}_controller.rb`, `api/v1/widget/*` (`base_controller.rb`, `configs_controller.rb`, `contacts_controller.rb`, `events_controller.rb`, `campaigns_controller.rb`, `inbox_members_controller.rb`). Guards: `concerns/website_token_helper.rb:8,14` (`@web_widget.inbox`), `concerns/ensure_current_account_helper.rb`. OAuth/channel callbacks: `instagram/`, `google/`, `tiktok/`, `twitter/callbacks_controller.rb`, `oauth_callback_controller.rb`. EE: `enterprise/app/controllers/enterprise/api/v1/accounts/inboxes_controller.rb:90`.

### D. Background work (jobs)
`app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb` (teardown — Step 3), `app/jobs/auto_assignment/assignment_job.rb:26` (`Inbox.find_by`), `app/jobs/inboxes/fetch_imap_email_inboxes_job.rb:6` (`Inbox.where(channel_type:)`), `app/jobs/inboxes/fetch_imap_emails_job.rb` (per-channel), `app/jobs/webhooks/whatsapp_events_job.rb:130`. EE: `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb`, `enterprise/app/jobs/captain/inbox_pending_conversations_resolution_job.rb:8`.

### E. Shared model behavior (concerns/services driven by the contract)
`AutoAssignment::AssignmentService` (`app/services/auto_assignment/assignment_service.rb:1`, `pattr_initialize [:inbox!]`), `RoundRobinSelector` (`round_robin_selector.rb:1`), `InboxRoundRobinService` (`inbox_round_robin_service.rb`), `RateLimiter`, `agent_assignment_service.rb`; `Reauthorizable` (`app/models/concerns/reauthorizable.rb:80-100` — `invalidate_inbox_cache`, `dispatch_inbox_reauthorization_event`). EE: `enterprise/app/services/enterprise/auto_assignment/assignment_service.rb`, `enterprise/app/services/enterprise/auto_assignment/capacity_service.rb`.

### F. Provider-specific services (per channel_type)
`app/services/instagram/base_message_text.rb:13-15`, `app/services/tiktok/{messaging_helpers,token_service}.rb`, `app/services/whatsapp/{phone_number_normalization,incoming_message_identifier_helper}.rb`, `app/services/contacts/contactable_inboxes_service.rb:6-24` (switches on `inbox.channel_type`), `app/services/message_templates/template/{greeting,out_of_office}.rb`, `app/services/conversations/unread_counts/broadcast_scope.rb:32`. EE: `enterprise/app/services/whatsapp/{call_service,incoming_call_service,call_permission_reply_service}.rb`.

### G. Builders / library / integration code
Builders: `app/builders/contact_inbox_builder.rb`, `contact_inbox_with_contact_builder.rb`, `conversation_builder.rb`, `campaigns/campaign_conversation_builder.rb`, `notification_builder.rb`, `email/{base,from,reply_to}_builder.rb`, `v2/reports/{outgoing_messages_count,channel_summary}_builder.rb`. Lib: `lib/integrations/google_translate/processor_service.rb:31`, `lib/captain/{follow_up_service,base_task_service}.rb`, `lib/tasks/download_report.rake:124`. Listeners: `app/listeners/{webhook,hook,agent_bot,notification,reporting_event,campaign,action_cable}_listener.rb` (e.g. `webhook_listener.rb:122` `inbox.channel.webhook_url`, `hook_listener.rb:43`, `agent_bot_listener.rb:74`).

### H. Paid-tier overlay (Enterprise)
Model overrides (`enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox.rb`, `.../concerns/inbox.rb`, `.../inbox_agent_availability.rb`, `.../audit/inbox.rb`, `.../channelable.rb:16`), audit log on teardown (`enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb`), Captain (`enterprise/app/services/captain/llm/*`, `enterprise/app/helpers/captain/chat_helper.rb:107`), SLA (`enterprise/app/services/sla/evaluate_applied_sla_service.rb`), capacity (`agent_capacity_policy`, `inbox_capacity_limit`).

### I. Policies / finders
`app/policies/inbox_policy.rb:1` (`user.assigned_inboxes` scope, `:14,:26`), plus `agent_bot_policy.rb`, `contact_policy.rb`; `app/finders/` (conversation finder filters by inbox).

---

## Step 5 — How inbox availability & identity are guarded in background work

**Assignment (per-inbox bulk job):**
- `AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob#perform` re-loads from DB and bails if gone: `inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id); return unless inbox` (`app/jobs/auto_assignment/assignment_job.rb:26-27`). Identity is also gated by a per-inbox Redis in-flight token so two runs can't double-assign (`:9-23, 44-49`).
- `AutoAssignment::AssignmentService#perform_bulk_assignment` guards the *current* config before acting: `return 0 unless inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled?` and `unless inbox.enable_auto_assignment?` (`app/services/auto_assignment/assignment_service.rb:5-6`), `return 0 if inbox.available_agents.empty?` (`:10`). Row-level claim uses `inbox.conversations … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (`:93-101`).
- `AutoAssignmentHandler#run_auto_assignment` (conversation `after_save`) re-checks live inbox state: `should_run_auto_assignment?` → `inbox.enable_auto_assignment?` + `inbox.members.exclude?(assignee)` (`app/models/concerns/auto_assignment_handler.rb:40-47`).
- Round-robin self-heals its queue against current membership: `validate_queue?` compares `inbox.inbox_members.map(&:user_id)` to the Redis list and resets if stale (`app/services/auto_assignment/inbox_round_robin_service.rb:28-53`); `InboxMember#remove_agent_from_round_robin` guards `if inbox.present?` because the inbox may already be torn down (`app/models/inbox_member.rb:35`).

**Fetching (IMAP email maintenance):**
- `Inboxes::FetchImapEmailInboxesJob` re-queries `Inbox.where(channel_type: 'Channel::Email')` each run (`app/jobs/inboxes/fetch_imap_email_inboxes_job.rb:6`) and per-inbox gates on `should_fetch_emails?`: account not suspended, `channel.imap_enabled`, **`!channel.reauthorization_required?`**, plus cloud/plan checks (`:14-22`).
- `Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob#should_fetch_email?` re-checks `channel.imap_enabled? && !channel.reauthorization_required?` (`app/jobs/inboxes/fetch_imap_emails_job.rb:27-29`) and serializes with a per-inbox Redis mutex keyed on `channel.inbox.id` (`:9-11`).

**Periodic maintenance / reauthorization identity:**
- `Reauthorizable` tracks per-object validity in Redis keyed by `table_name.singularize:id` (`app/models/concerns/reauthorizable.rb:103-109`); `reauthorization_required?` reads that key (`:19-21`); on threshold breach it flips the flag, invalidates the inbox cache (`invalidate_inbox_cache`, guarded `if inbox.present?`, `:99-101`) and dispatches an inbox event guarded by `respond_to?(:inbox)` + `inbox.blank?` (`:80-85`).
- Provider services confirm a live, authorized channel before sending: `instagram/base_message_text.rb:13-15` (`return if @inbox.blank?` then `channel.reauthorization_required?`), `tiktok/token_service.rb:12`.

**Summary of what's checked:** existence (`find_by … return unless`), current config (`enable_auto_assignment?`, `auto_assignment_v2_enabled?`, `imap_enabled`), account state (`suspended?`), channel auth validity (`reauthorization_required?`), live membership (round-robin queue revalidation), and concurrency identity (per-inbox Redis tokens/mutexes).

---

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

You are changing **teardown behavior and a couple of associations' `dependent:` semantics**. Risk ranking:

**🔴 Highest — silent data orphaning / job storms**
1. `DeleteObjectJob` heavy-association map (`app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb:18-23`) + EE override (`enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb:4-8`). If you change which Inbox associations are `:destroy_async` vs purged inline, the pre-purge list and the AR cascade can **double-delete or miss** records. This is the single most likely thing to break and the hardest to notice.
2. Account teardown via `account.rb:83` (`inboxes destroy_async`) — any change to Inbox cascade timing multiplies across all inboxes in an account; ordering between account-level pre-purge (conversations/contacts) and inbox-level cascade matters.
3. `Channelable has_one :inbox, dependent: :destroy_async, touch:` (`app/models/concerns/channelable.rb:6`) + `inbox.rb:60` bidirectional `dependent: :destroy`. A destroy from the channel side bypasses `DeleteObjectJob`'s pre-purge entirely — easy to create an inconsistency if you only harden the controller path.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Chatwoot, a Rails (backend) + Vue 3 (frontend) customer-engagement / shared-inbox platform. Standard Rails layout under `app/` (`models`, `controllers`, `services`, `jobs`, `listeners`, `builders`, `finders`, `policies`, `dispatchers`), library code under `lib/`, and a **paid-tier Enterprise overlay under `enterprise/`** that mirrors the same tree and extends OSS via `prepend_mod_with`/`include_mod_with`. Frontend lives in `app/javascript`. Specs in `spec/` (OSS) and `spec/enterprise/`.

**The model you're reworking — `Inbox`** — `app/models/inbox.rb:42`. An Inbox is the join between an **external messaging channel** (`belongs_to :channel, polymorphic: true, dependent: :destroy`, `inbox.rb:60`; the 12 channel classes live in `app/models/channel/`) and the rest of the system: conversations, contacts, messages, members all hang off it. It belongs to an `Account` (`inbox.rb:57`) and optionally a `Portal` (`inbox.rb:58`).

**Shared pieces it is built on (the concerns it includes):**
- `Reportable` — `app/models/concerns/reportable.rb:3` (adds `has_many :reporting_events`)
- `Avatarable` — `app/models/concerns/avatarable.rb:3`

- `AccountCacheRevalidator` — `app/models/concerns/account_cache_revalidator.rb:1`
- `InboxAgentAvailability` — `app/models/concerns/inbox_agent_availability.rb:1`

**Enterprise overlay of the model:** `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox.rb:1`, `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/concerns/inbox.rb:1` (adds `captain_inbox`, `inbox_capacity_limits`, `calls`), `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/audit/inbox.rb:1`, wired at `inbox.rb:269-271`.

## Step 2 — The Inbox contract and what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/inbox.rb:42`.

**Concerns it pulls in (shared behavior):**
| Concern | file:line | What it injects |
|---|---|---|
| `Reportable` | `app/models/concerns/reportable.rb:7` | `has_many :reporting_events, dependent: :destroy` |
| `Avatarable` | `app/models/concerns/avatarable.rb:10` | `has_one_attached :avatar`, gravatar fetch on save |
| `OutOfOffisable` | `app/models/concerns/out_of_offisable.rb:9-10` | `has_many :working_hours, dependent: :destroy_async`; `after_create :create_default_working_hours` |
| `AccountCacheRevalidator` | `app/models/concerns/account_cache_revalidator.rb:5` | `after_commit :update_account_cache` on create/update/**destroy** |
| `InboxAgentAvailability` | `app/models/concerns/inbox_agent_availability.rb` | `available_agents`, `member_ids_with_assignment_capacity` (overridden in EE at `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox_agent_availability.rb:4`) |

**Associations other records hang off it** (`inbox.rb:57-76`):
- `belongs_to :channel, polymorphic:, dependent: :destroy` (`:60`) — and the reverse `Channelable` side declares `has_one :inbox, as: :channel, dependent: :destroy_async, touch: true` (`app/models/concerns/channelable.rb:6`).
- `has_many :campaigns, :contact_inboxes, :conversations, :messages, :inbox_members, :webhooks, :hooks` — **all `dependent: :destroy_async`** (`:62-76`).
- `has_one :inbox_assignment_policy, dependent: :destroy` (`:71`), `has_one :agent_bot_inbox, dependent: :destroy_async` (`:73`).
- `has_many :contacts, through: :contact_inboxes` (`:64`), `has_many :members, through: :inbox_members` (`:67`).
- EE adds `has_one :captain_inbox, dependent: :destroy`, `has_many :inbox_capacity_limits, dependent: :destroy`, `has_many :calls, dependent: :destroy_async` (`enterprise/app/models/enterprise/concerns/inbox.rb:5-10`).

**Lifecycle callbacks (part of the contract):**
- `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` → clears the Redis round-robin queue (`inbox.rb:80, 260-262`).
- `after_create_commit :dispatch_create_event` / `after_update_commit :dispatch_update_event` (`inbox.rb:82-83`), plus `dispatch_reauthorization_event` (`inbox.rb:212`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** teardown is split across **three mechanisms with different timing** — `:destroy` (inline, synchronous: channel, `inbox_assignment_policy`), `:destroy_async` (enqueued per-record: conversations, messages, contact_inboxes, campaigns, members, webhooks, hooks, working_hours, agent_bot_inbox, calls), and an `after_destroy` Redis side-effect. `reporting_events` is `:destroy` via the concern but **also batch-purged by `DeleteObjectJob`** (see Step 3). The channel relationship is **bidirectional `dependent: :destroy`** — destroying either side cascades to the other, so the entry point matters.

## Step 3 — Teardown trace (inbox + attached data)

**Entry points (where destroy is triggered):**
1. **Single inbox deletion** — `app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/inboxes_controller.rb:77-78`: `DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(@inbox, Current.user, request.ip)`.
2. **Account teardown** — `app/controllers/super_admin/accounts_controller.rb:60-63`: `DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(account)` (account `has_many :inboxes, dependent: :destroy_async`, `app/models/account.rb:83`).
3. **Channel deletion** cascades to its inbox via `Channelable` `has_one :inbox, dependent: :destroy_async` (`app/models/concerns/channelable.rb:6`).

**Layers, in order:**
1. `DeleteObjectJob#perform` — `app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb:6-12`. Runs on the `:low` queue.
2. `purge_heavy_associations` — `delete_object_job.rb:25-34`. For an `Inbox`, **inline batch-destroys** `conversations`, `contact_inboxes`, `reporting_events` in batches of 5,000 (`heavy_associations` map `:18-23`, `batch_destroy` `:36-40`). This pre-empties the largest tables synchronously *before* the model destroy, to avoid `destroy_async` fan-out timeouts/races. (For an `Account` it purges `conversations, contacts, inboxes, reporting_events`; EE adds `SlaPolicy => [:applied_slas]` at `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb:4-8`.)
3. `object.destroy!` — `delete_object_job.rb:10`. Fires AR `dependent:` cascades:
   - **Inline (`:destroy`)**: `channel` (`inbox.rb:60`), `inbox_assignment_policy` (`:71`), and EE `captain_inbox`/`inbox_capacity_limits` (`enterprise/.../concerns/inbox.rb:5,9`), plus `reporting_events` (`reportable.rb:7`).
   - **Async (`:destroy_async`, enqueued one job per association)**: `campaigns, contact_inboxes, conversations, messages, inbox_members, webhooks, hooks` (`inbox.rb:62-76`), `agent_bot_inbox` (`:73`), `working_hours` (`out_of_offisable.rb:9`), EE `calls`.
   - `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` → `AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService#clear_queue` deletes the Redis key inline (`inbox.rb:260-262`, `app/services/auto_assignment/inbox_round_robin_service.rb:5-7`).
   - `after_commit :update_account_cache` bumps the account cache key (`account_cache_revalidator.rb:5-9`).
4. `process_post_deletion_tasks` — `delete_object_job.rb:11,14` (no-op in OSS). EE override writes an `Enterprise::AuditLog` `destroy` entry for Inbox/Conversation/SlaPolicy (`enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb:10-25`).

**Async vs inline:** Inline = the heavy pre-purge (conversations/contact_inboxes/reporting_events), the polymorphic `channel`, `inbox_assignment_policy`, EE captain/capacity, and the Redis queue clear. Async (separate jobs) = messages, members, campaigns, webhooks, hooks, agent_bot_inbox, working_hours, calls, and the *normal* (non-pre-purged) path for contact_inboxes/conversations when destroyed outside `DeleteObjectJob`.

## Step 5 — How inbox availability & identity are guarded in background work

**Assignment (per-inbox bulk job):**
- `AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob#perform` re-loads from DB and bails if gone: `inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id); return unless inbox` (`app/jobs/auto_assignment/assignment_job.rb:26-27`). Identity is also gated by a per-inbox Redis in-flight token so two runs can't double-assign (`:9-23, 44-49`).
- `AutoAssignment::AssignmentService#perform_bulk_assignment` guards the *current* config before acting: `return 0 unless inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled?` and `unless inbox.enable_auto_assignment?` (`app/services/auto_assignment/assignment_service.rb:5-6`), `return 0 if inbox.available_agents.empty?` (`:10`). Row-level claim uses `inbox.conversations … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (`:93-101`).
- `AutoAssignmentHandler#run_auto_assignment` (conversation `after_save`) re-checks live inbox state: `should_run_auto_assignment?` → `inbox.enable_auto_assignment?` + `inbox.members.exclude?(assignee)` (`app/models/concerns/auto_assignment_handler.rb:40-47`).
- Round-robin self-heals its queue against current membership: `validate_queue?` compares `inbox.inbox_members.map(&:user_id)` to the Redis list and resets if stale (`app/services/auto_assignment/inbox_round_robin_service.rb:28-53`); `InboxMember#remove_agent_from_round_robin` guards `if inbox.present?` because the inbox may already be torn down (`app/models/inbox_member.rb:35`).

**Fetching (IMAP email maintenance):**
- `Inboxes::FetchImapEmailInboxesJob` re-queries `Inbox.where(channel_type: 'Channel::Email')` each run (`app/jobs/inboxes/fetch_imap_email_inboxes_job.rb:6`) and per-inbox gates on `should_fetch_emails?`: account not suspended, `channel.imap_enabled`, **`!channel.reauthorization_required?`**, plus cloud/plan checks (`:14-22`).
- `Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob#should_fetch_email?` re-checks `channel.imap_enabled? && !channel.reauthorization_required?` (`app/jobs/inboxes/fetch_imap_emails_job.rb:27-29`) and serializes with a per-inbox Redis mutex keyed on `channel.inbox.id` (`:9-11`).

**Periodic maintenance / reauthorization identity:**
- `Reauthorizable` tracks per-object validity in Redis keyed by `table_name.singularize:id` (`app/models/concerns/reauthorizable.rb:103-109`); `reauthorization_required?` reads that key (`:19-21`); on threshold breach it flips the flag, invalidates the inbox cache (`invalidate_inbox_cache`, guarded `if inbox.present?`, `:99-101`) and dispatches an inbox event guarded by `respond_to?(:inbox)` + `inbox.blank?` (`:80-85`).
- Provider services confirm a live, authorized channel before sending: `instagram/base_message_text.rb:13-15` (`return if @inbox.blank?` then `channel.reauthorization_required?`), `tiktok/token_service.rb:12`.

**Summary of what's checked:** existence (`find_by … return unless`), current config (`enable_auto_assignment?`, `auto_assignment_v2_enabled?`, `imap_enabled`), account state (`suspended?`), channel auth validity (`reauthorization_required?`), live membership (round-robin queue revalidation), and concurrency identity (per-inbox Redis tokens/mutexes).

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

You are changing **teardown behavior and a couple of associations' `dependent:` semantics**. Risk ranking:

**🔴 Highest — silent data orphaning / job storms**
1. `DeleteObjectJob` heavy-association map (`app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb:18-23`) + EE override (`enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb:4-8`). If you change which Inbox associations are `:destroy_async` vs purged inline, the pre-purge list and the AR cascade can **double-delete or miss** records. This is the single most likely thing to break and the hardest to notice.
2. Account teardown via `account.rb:83` (`inboxes destroy_async`) — any change to Inbox cascade timing multiplies across all inboxes in an account; ordering between account-level pre-purge (conversations/contacts) and inbox-level cascade matters.
3. `Channelable has_one :inbox, dependent: :destroy_async, touch:` (`app/models/concerns/channelable.rb:6`) + `inbox.rb:60` bidirectional `dependent: :destroy`. A destroy from the channel side bypasses `DeleteObjectJob`'s pre-purge entirely — easy to create an inconsistency if you only harden the controller path.

**🟠 High — FK integrity on associations whose `dependent:` you touch**
4. `belongs_to :inbox` without `optional:` and `inbox_id NOT NULL`: `conversation.rb:101`, `message.rb:129`, `contact_inbox.rb:32`, `working_hour.rb:24`, `agent_bot_inbox.rb:19`, `inbox_member.rb:23`, EE `call.rb:46`, `captain_inbox.rb:19`. If a parent record survives teardown they become invalid/orphaned.
5. `portal.rb:38` `dependent: :nullify` — different semantic from the rest; portals must *survive* inbox deletion. Don't accidentally convert to destroy.
6. `reporting_events` is declared `dependent: :destroy` (`reportable.rb:7`) **and** batch-purged (`delete_object_job.rb:21`) — duplicated cleanup; changing one without the other is a trap.

**🟡 Medium — behavioral side-effects on destroy**
7. `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` (`inbox.rb:80,260`) + `InboxMember#remove_agent_from_round_robin` guard (`inbox_member.rb:35`) — Redis queue cleanup; reordering callbacks can leave stale queues.
8. `AccountCacheRevalidator after_commit on :destroy` (`account_cache_revalidator.rb:5`) — needs `account` still loadable at commit.
9. EE audit on teardown (`enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb:10-25`) reads `object.attributes` / `object.account` — fragile if attributes/associations change.

**🟢 Lower — read-path dependents to re-verify (won't corrupt, may error)**
10. Assignment background path (`assignment_job.rb:26`, `assignment_service.rb:5-10`, `auto_assignment_handler.rb`) — already guards existence; verify guards still hold if association names change.
11. IMAP fetch jobs (`fetch_imap_email_inboxes_job.rb`, `fetch_imap_emails_job.rb`) — re-query each run; low risk.
12. Provider services / builders / listeners / lib (Step 4 F/G) — they read `inbox.channel*`; only at risk if the channel association semantics change.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** the full `DeleteObjectJob` Inbox + Account paths (sync queue, no orphans), channel-initiated destroy, round-robin Redis cleanup, account cache bump, EE audit-log creation, EE captain/capacity/calls cascade, and portal nullify behavior.
